channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: How enterprises are scaling AI
item.description: OpenAI's new enterprise resource maps the path from early AI experiments to compounding business impact, covering trust-building, governance frameworks, workflow design, and quality-at-scale. The guide draws on patterns from OpenAI's largest enterprise deployments and is aimed at organisations moving from pilot to production. It covers how to select the right model for a task, manage responsible deployment, and measure ROI across business functions.

item.title: 'Your Career Starts at the Beginning of the AI Revolution,' NVIDIA CEO Tells Graduates
item.description: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang delivered the keynote at Carnegie Mellon University's 128th commencement, telling graduates they are entering the world at 'an extraordinary moment' as a new AI industry is born. Huang positioned the current era — a new era of science and discovery — as generational, urging new engineers and scientists to embrace their role in building it. The speech signals NVIDIA's continued push to frame AI infrastructure as a foundational societal layer, not just a technology cycle.

item.title: NZ Optometrists Board issues AI guidelines for eyecare practice
item.description: The Optometrists and Dispensing Opticians Board (ODOB) released guidelines for AI use in NZ optometry, aligning with UK approaches and pointing practitioners to the Privacy Commissioner's resources. Local research includes Dr. James Lewis training a neural network on 27,000+ ocular images to detect ptosis, with the article flagging equity and bias risks in datasets like OCT analysis.

item.title: Experian: 40% of data breaches in 2025 were AI-powered
item.description: Experian reports 40% of the 5,000 data breaches it serviced in 2025 involved AI-powered tactics, and forecasts agentic AI will drive a fresh wave of identity theft and scams through 2026. The piece urges firms to harden authentication and synthetic-media detection ahead of agent-driven fraud at scale.

item.title: OncoAgent: A Dual-Tier Multi-Agent Framework for Privacy-Preserving Oncology Clinical Decision Support
item.description: OncoAgent proposes a dual-tier multi-agent architecture for oncology clinical decision support, splitting reasoning across an on-device tier for sensitive patient data and a cloud tier for general medical knowledge. The paper claims competitive accuracy on cancer-staging benchmarks while keeping protected health information off third-party model servers.

item.title: Greg Brockman highlights GPT-Realtime-2 powering live audio translation in browser
item.description: OpenAI president Greg Brockman amplified a demo of GPT-Realtime-2 driving real-time audio translation inside Chrome — usable on YouTube, livestreams and meetings. The post signals continued investment in OpenAI's realtime voice stack and adjacent third-party agent integrations.

item.title: Sam Altman opens public feedback on next OpenAI flagship model
item.description: Sam Altman asked X what users most want improved in the next OpenAI flagship, framing the post as part of the planning loop for the o-series successor. The thread drew thousands of responses and is being used as a soft signal for capability priorities.

item.title: FIS partners with Anthropic on AI for anti-money-laundering investigations
item.description: Financial-services platform FIS announced a partnership with Anthropic to deploy Claude-powered agents for anti-money-laundering investigations and case triage. The pitch targets banks looking to compress AML alert backlogs while keeping human-in-the-loop adjudication.

item.title: NZ Public Service AI Toolkit adds policy template + AI records-management guidance
item.description: New Zealand's digital.govt.nz refreshed its Public Service AI Toolkit with a customisable AI policy template for agencies and new guidance on records management for AI systems. The update aligns with MBIE's 2025 national AI strategy and gives public-sector teams a documented starting point for responsible AI adoption.

item.title: RBNZ flags advanced AI cyber risk to NZ borrowers and lenders
item.description: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand says advanced AI models capable of automating cyber attacks could amplify operational risk to borrowers and regulated entities, threatening loan repayments. RBNZ is coordinating with peer agencies including the Reserve Bank of Australia to monitor the threat surface.

item.title: Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0
item.description: xAI has released Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0, a new voice model designed for low-latency real-time conversational AI. The model is optimized for speed-first voice interactions where responsiveness matters more than extended reasoning, complementing xAI’s existing voice lineup.

item.title: How Alta Daily Uses Meta’s Segment Anything to Reimagine the Digital Closet
item.description: Fashion startup Alta Daily has integrated Meta’s Segment Anything Model into its digital closet app, enabling one-click garment segmentation and background removal at production scale. The case study shows how SAM’s zero-shot segmentation generalises to consumer applications beyond the research setting.

item.title: Akamai surges on big LLM deal — Anthropic signs $1.8B edge-inference contract, largest in Akamai history
item.description: Anthropic has signed a seven-year, $1.8 billion infrastructure deal with Akamai to run Claude inference across 4,300 edge locations in 130 countries — the largest contract in Akamai’s 27-year history. The deal reflects Anthropic’s strategy to distribute inference beyond hyperscale cloud providers; Akamai stock surged 26% on the announcement.

item.title: CyberSecQwen-4B: Why Defensive Cyber Needs Small, Specialized, Locally-Runnable Models
item.description: CyberSecQwen-4B is a 4-billion parameter security-focused model from the AMD developer hackathon, optimized for local deployment on edge hardware. It demonstrates that small, specialized models can outperform larger general-purpose models on defensive cyber benchmarks without requiring cloud infrastructure or data exfiltration risks.

item.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
item.description: NVIDIA researchers demonstrate grammar-constrained decoding to substantially improve bash script generation in small language models, enabling reliable shell command output without fine-tuning on proprietary data. The approach enforces syntactic validity at inference time, cutting hallucinated flags and invalid pipelines.

item.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
item.description: AllenAI introduces EMO (Emergent Modularity via Pretraining), a mixture-of-experts approach where modules emerge naturally during pretraining without explicit routing supervision. The method shows improved specialisation and benchmark performance versus dense baselines.

item.title: Streaming Tokens and Tools: Multi-Turn Agentic Harness Support in NVIDIA Dynamo
item.description: NVIDIA Dynamo now supports streaming tokens and multi-turn tool calls in agentic harnesses, allowing assistant reasoning turns to interleave with tool invocations and return structured results across conversation turns. This directly enables production-grade agent loops on Dynamo inference infrastructure.

item.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
item.description: Google launched The Small Brief, an initiative where ad industry veterans use Google AI to create breakthrough advertising campaigns for small local businesses, demonstrating AI-assisted creative production at the agency level.

item.title: OpenAI: Running Codex safely
item.description: OpenAI documents the controls it ships for safer Codex deployments, covering sandboxed execution, approval workflows for destructive actions, network egress policies and agent-specific telemetry. The post is aimed at platform teams onboarding coding agents who need a defensible baseline for production use.

item.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
item.description: NVIDIA has deployed OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 to power Codex across its own engineering workflows, with internal teams using the AI coding agent to accelerate software development at scale. The blog details how GPT-5.5’s enhanced agentic capabilities make it the backbone of Codex’s latest production release on NVIDIA’s infrastructure.

item.title: MedQA: Fine-Tuning a Clinical AI on AMD ROCm — No CUDA Required
item.description: A hackathon team writes up fine-tuning a clinical question-answering model on AMD MI300X GPUs using ROCm, demonstrating a CUDA-free path for medical-domain LLMs. The post documents reproducible training scripts and a small evaluation harness for clinical tasks.

item.title: AI avatars to deliver real-time NZ Sign Language translations
item.description: An NZ-led project is nearing completion on AI avatars that produce real-time New Zealand Sign Language translations, aimed at situations where a live interpreter is unavailable. The Deaf community has been involved in design, and trials are planned across public services.

item.title: NZ organisations accelerate AI adoption amid growing resilience and governance gaps
item.description: A new report on New Zealand enterprises finds AI is being deployed faster than security and governance practices can keep up, especially in cybersecurity tooling and customer operations. The authors call for board-level AI risk frameworks and reporting standards.

item.title: Achieving Peak System and Workload Efficiency on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 with Slurm Block Scheduling
item.description: NVIDIA's GB200 NVL72 rack achieves peak efficiency via Slurm block scheduling that aligns GPU NVLink coherence zones with job boundaries, reducing cross-rack communication overhead for large training runs.

item.title: Model Quantization: Post-Training Quantization Using NVIDIA Model Optimizer
item.description: NVIDIA Model Optimizer enables post-training quantization (PTQ) to run large models on consumer RTX GPUs with reduced VRAM and faster inference, covering INT8/INT4/FP8 recipes for popular architectures.

item.title: OpenAI previews GPT-5.5-Cyber for critical-infrastructure defenders
item.description: OpenAI president Greg Brockman announced a limited preview of GPT-5.5-Cyber, a security-tuned variant of GPT-5.5 designed to help critical-infrastructure operators detect and patch code vulnerabilities. It ships with the Trusted Access for Cyber programme for vetted defenders.

item.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
item.description: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck discussed AI's role in powering America's energy future at the SCSP AI+ Expo, arguing that AI-driven demand requires and will accelerate new energy infrastructure investment.

item.title: Grok Imagine Quality Mode API
item.description: xAI exposed Grok Imagine Quality Mode through the API, giving developers programmatic access to the higher-fidelity image generation tier previously limited to the Grok app. Pricing is per-image at the higher quality cost band.

item.title: Connectors now on Grok Web
item.description: xAI rolled connectors out to Grok on the web, letting users link Gmail, Google Drive, GitHub, and other services so Grok can search and act across them. Mirrors agent-style integrations already shipped by ChatGPT and Claude.

item.title: Andrew Ng + CopilotKit: course on building UI-generating AI agents
item.description: Andrew Ng announced a DeepLearning.AI short course with CopilotKit on building agents that generate dynamic UIs — charts, forms, whiteboards — directly inside chat. The course covers component-based, compositional, and third-party app integration patterns.
